name: Web UI surface > Node > Processes > Agents should have a stop button next to all

failed_actions.message: The web route directly orchestrates chat lookup, process termination, and Goal cancellation instead of delegating this product behavior to a shared capability, making the surface non-thin.

Stop-agent-and-cancel-Goal semantics exist only in the web server route; the shared process service still performs process-only signaling, so there is no authoritative implementation of the combined operation.
failed_actions.rule: Compose existing capabilities with one authoritative implementation per concern.

failed_actions.message: The stop path uses FileProcessSupervisor::signal, which removes the process record immediately after requesting termination, then marks the Goal cancelled without confirming the process exited. A termination-resistant agent can remain alive but untracked under a cancelled Goal.
failed_actions.rule: Keep long-running work supervised with explicit status, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: The tests cover a cooperative sleep process only and assert Goal cancellation before checking eventual exit; there is no failure-path evidence for a process that ignores or delays termination, despite changing process cancellation and recovery semantics.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to architectural and operational risk.

failed_actions.message: The browser/API path now cancels a linked Goal, while CLI system ps --stop still invokes the shared signal-only service and does not cancel the Goal or handle synthetic chat-agent processes, producing inconsistent process-stop semantics across surfaces.
failed_actions.rule: Preserve one capability across UI, CLI, API, and MCP.

verdict.message: Agent stopping is surface-specific and can cancel a Goal before confirming process exit.

violations.message: The web route directly orchestrates chat lookup, process termination, and Goal cancellation instead of delegating this product behavior to a shared capability, making the surface non-thin.

Stop-agent-and-cancel-Goal semantics exist only in the web server route; the shared process service still performs process-only signaling, so there is no authoritative implementation of the combined operation.
violations.rule: Compose existing capabilities with one authoritative implementation per concern.

violations.message: The stop path uses FileProcessSupervisor::signal, which removes the process record immediately after requesting termination, then marks the Goal cancelled without confirming the process exited. A termination-resistant agent can remain alive but untracked under a cancelled Goal.
violations.rule: Keep long-running work supervised with explicit status, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

violations.message: The tests cover a cooperative sleep process only and assert Goal cancellation before checking eventual exit; there is no failure-path evidence for a process that ignores or delays termination, despite changing process cancellation and recovery semantics.
violations.rule: Require evidence proportional to architectural and operational risk.

violations.message: The browser/API path now cancels a linked Goal, while CLI system ps --stop still invokes the shared signal-only service and does not cancel the Goal or handle synthetic chat-agent processes, producing inconsistent process-stop semantics across surfaces.
violations.rule: Preserve one capability across UI, CLI, API, and MCP.

rounds.implementation_report: Added a Stop button to every active Agents row and unified it on POST /api/processes/:id/stop; managed, interactive, and chat agent stops now terminate or close the selected agent, and Goal-linked stops persist and return the Goal as cancelled. Updated process intent and added UI/server regression coverage. Verification: cargo test --lib: 455 passed, 0 failed; node --test tests/*.test.js: 36 passed, 0 failed; cargo check --all-targets: passed; cargo fmt -- --check: passed; git diff --check: passed.
rounds.prompt: Web UI surface > Node > Processes > Agents should have a stop button next to all the agents to kill the process and move the Goal into the cancelled state.

Recover rejected candidate a1f6c2e6 from current main while preserving that candidate, its worktree, and all prior provider and Governance evidence. Fix only the five recorded Governance violations. Move the combined stop-agent-and-cancel-linked-Goal behavior into one authoritative shared product capability used consistently by browser, API, CLI system process stop, and MCP surfaces, leaving each surface thin and supporting managed, interactive, chat, and synthetic agent records. Do not remove supervision evidence or mark a linked Goal cancelled merely after requesting termination: retain the process record, confirm the exact owned process has exited, then persist cancellation; if termination is delayed, resisted, mismatched, or fails, keep the Goal non-cancelled and return visible causal failure and recovery evidence. Add focused shared-service and cross-surface coverage, including a termination-resistant or delayed process, identity mismatch, successful confirmed exit before Goal cancellation, and consistent CLI/API/browser behavior. Preserve authentication, cancellation, provider, worktree, Governance, Quality, and human Review boundaries. Do not force merge, rewrite history, discard worktrees, hide failures, or edit durable Goal state directly. Re-run focused process tests, the full library and web suites, all-target check, formatting, and diff checks, and report exact results.
